title: Detection of Trypanosoma cruzi by Indirect Methods: IIF, ELISA, and IHA
author: Claudia Méndez
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.62khgcw

steps.title: Antigen-Antibody formation
steps.instruction: Antigen-Antibody formation: The IIF staining was performed using antigen glass slides impregnated with trypomastigote forms of Chagas obtained from culture in house. A volume of 12 µL of the serum patients and controls (previously diluted with buffer in serial dilutions in base 2 from

steps.title: 256), were deposited in the wells...
steps.instruction: 256), were deposited in the wells with antigen, followed by incubation at 37 °C and finally they were washed.

steps.title: Detection of Antigen-Antibody
steps.instruction: Detection of Antigen-Antibody: An anti-human IgG labeled with fluorescein and Evans Blue was used as a conjugate, it was incubated and washed again.

steps.title: Reading
steps.instruction: Reading: After another washing step, allowed to dry at room temperature and it was observed under fluorescence microscopy. Samples with fluorescence greater than or equal to 1:32 were considered positive.

steps.title: Antigen complex antibody formation
steps.instruction: Antigen complex antibody formation: Diluted patient serum at 37 °C and the corresponding kit controls were incubated on the antigen-coated plates, which, if the respective antibodies were present, formed a stable complex that was not removed by subsequent washing.

steps.title: Complex reaction
steps.instruction: Complex reaction: 100 µL of conjugate was added, which consists of a peroxidase-labeled human anti IgG, which one bound to the complex after the respective incubation.

steps.title: Detection
steps.instruction: Detection: After washing, 100 µL of conjugate reacting with the peroxidase was added, revealing color, proportional to the amount of complex formed in each of the wells, by incubation time at room temperature. The reaction was stopped by adding sulfuric acid.

steps.title: Reading
steps.instruction: Reading: The color intensity was measured in a colorimetric reader for ELISA plates with a 450 nm filter.

steps.title: Interpretation
steps.instruction: Interpretation: • ELISA CHAGAS III GRUPOBIOS SA TESTCut off calculation: (average of positive controls + average of negative controls) * 0.35 POSITIVE: When the absorbance of the sample is greater than cut off + 10%NEGATIVE: When the absorbance of the sample is less than cut off + 10%DOUBTFUL: When the absorbance of the sample is in a range of cut off +/- 10% • ELISA CHAGAS T.cruzi Ab DIAPRO TESTCut-off value: average absorbance of negative controls + 0.35Patient results: patient sample absorbance / cut-off value POSITIVE: 1.1NEGATIVE: <0.9DOUBTFUL: 0.9 - 1.1

steps.title: Elimination of heterolipic antibodies
steps.instruction: Elimination of heterolipic antibodies: In order to eliminate nonspecific interfering antibodies that may interfere with the agglutination of red blood cells, 25 µL of patients and controls serum were incubated with 25 µL of 2% mercaptoethanol.

steps.title: Dilutions
steps.instruction: Dilutions: Serial dilutions were made on the basis of ½ to 1/64 from the first well diluted with mercaptoethanol.

steps.title: Heterophilia control
steps.instruction: Heterophilia control: 25 µL of non-sensitized red blood cells were added in the dilutions ½ and ¼.

steps.title: Reactions
steps.instruction: Reactions: 25 µL of IHA antigen was added to the other dilutions, corresponding to ram red blood cells sensitized with antigens with cytoplasmic antigens of T. cruzi.

steps.title: Reading
steps.instruction: Reading: After waiting for 90 minutes at room temperature, the visualization was performed. Formation of a mantle of at least 50% on the surface of the well was interpreted as positive and a small agglutination of irregular edges as negative result.
